Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez, via Zoom, for Kathy Liu, Beatrice Hsu, and Jerry Liu, Petitioners.

Counsel advises of the Notice of Settlement filed with the court. The court advises of the unacceptable road map of this matter and of the issue with opposing counsel not appearing today. Mr. Rodriguez reports of having planned to file a Petition to Approve the Settlement Agreement but intends to file a Request for Dismissal within the next few weeks. The court advises that it is willing to continue this matter to allow for the filing of either a Petition to Approve the Settlement Agreement or a Request for Dismissal. Counsel reports that consents may be filed and if they are, he does not intend to file a Petition to Approve the Settlement Agreement.

The matters are continued to Jul-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on the Filing of a Request for Dismissal or a Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement is set for the same date.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Monsour, via zoom, for Kathy N. Liu, Beatrice Hsu and Jerry C. Liu, Petitioners; Daniel Bernhard, via zoom, for Chung-Wen Liu, Patrick Liu, and Jonathan Liu, Respondents; Josh Davis, via zoom, for Chuang Mei Liu, Objector.

Mr. Davis provides oral testimony regarding what caused him to miss the previous calling of this matter which prompted the court to issue the Order to Show Cause which is set for hearing today. The court withdraws the Order to Show Cause and queries counsel regarding whether there is anything left to discuss today. Mr. Monsour asks whether the court will order the parties to participate in any form of ADR. The court advises Mr. Monsour that it will not be ordering the parties to ADR at this time but encourages the parties to engage in settlement discussions before the next calling of this matter, for trial setting.

The Order to Show Cause is withdrawn/taken off calendar. The Petition for Order, etc., is continued to Jun-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez, via ZOOM, for Kathy Liu, Jerry Liu, and Beatrice Hsu, Petitioners; Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, for Chung-Wen Liu, Patrick Liu, and Jonathan Liu, Respondents.

The court notes the absence of Mr. Davis, counsel for Chuang Mei Liu, Objector. Mr. Rodriguez reports that the last time he communicated with Mr. Davis, Mr. Davis advised him that he could not contact his client. Mr. Rodriguez reports that a Discovery referee has been hired and points to the Motion to Compel Chuang Mei Liu's deposition which is currently set for May-20-2022. The court mentions the issue regarding Mr. Davis' absence at today's hearing. Mr. Bernhard reports on Chuang Mei Liu's age and that she may not find herself in good health. Mr. Rodriguez points to the possible change in landscape of this matter if Chuang Mei Liu's health declines and she passes away. The court advises counsel that they should continue to work on discovery to move this matter along despite Chuang Mei Liu's position and urges counsel to discuss ADR options prior to trial. The court suggests Mr. Rodriguez consider petitioning the court to advance the motion set for May-20-2022.

After additional discussion, the court advises that it will be issuing an Order to Show Cause to Attorney Josh Davis and Objector Chuang Mei Liu to appear on Mar-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to explain why they did not appear and to explain why the court should not impose sanctions in the amount of $500 each.

The Petition for Order, etc., is continued to Mar-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Jun-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Continued To Mar-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Mar-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, for Chung-Wei Liu, Patrick Liu, and Jonathan Liu, Respondents; Kevin Rodriguez, via ZOOM, for Kathy Liu, Beatrice Hsu, and Jerry Liu, Petitioners; Josh Davis, via ZOOM, for Chuang Mei Liu, Objector.

The court begins by thanking the parties for submitting a thorough Case Management Statement. The court questions counsel regarding trial readiness as it appears that there are quite a few pending depositions, and none have been noticed. The court reminds the parties that it is up to them to have the matter ready for trial and that the court may not be inclined to grant a request to continue the trial as it has been almost four years since this matter was filed. The court then questions whether the children of the settlor, who may be contingent beneficiaries, have been notified. Mr. Rodriguez offers oral testimony regarding his belief that the settlor's children are not required to be notified. The court disagrees and advises that the children are to be provided notice by the next calling of this matter, to avoid any unforeseen, future notice issues. Regarding the delays in discovery, Mr. Bernhard reports that since Ms. Liu's unfortunate, sudden health related issues, it has been difficult to schedule her deposition. The court acknowledges Ms. Liu's current condition and advises counsel that the appointment of a guardian ad litem for Ms. Liu should be a priority. The court queries counsel if they have considered participating in ADR and if not, that the court is likely to order the matter to some form of ADR. Mr. Bernhard advises that he is an advocate of ADR and that he would be wiling to do so. Mr. Rodriguez confirms that the trust is valued at more than the limit allowed to participate in the court's pro bono mediation program. The court instructs the parties to meet and confer regarding ADR and that it will be expecting a robust response regarding ADR by the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the court confirms that the Mar-01-2022 date - previously set for trial - will now be a Case Management Hearing Date. The Mar-01-2022 trial date is continued to Jun-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Petition is continued to Mar-01-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Mr. Rodriguez is instructed to submit a proposed order to the court with the new trial and Case Management Hearing dates. Mr. Rodriguez is instructed to monitor the court's website for the order to print it and serve it on all the parties, including the settlor's children. The court asks counsel to attempt to stipulate to the extension of discovery cut-off dates.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Mar-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for Jerry Liu, Beatrice Hsu and Kathy Liu (Petitioners).
Daniel Bernhard appeared by zoom for Patrick Liu, Jonathan Liu and Edward Liu (Respondents).
Josh Davis appeared by zoom for Chuang Mei Liu (Respondent).

The court thanked counsel for the joint case management statement. Mr. Rodriguez stated that they are proceeding with discovery, that there is a wrath of depositions to be taken in the next five months, that they are discussing the potential use of a discovery referee and asked the court to issue an order. The Court informed the parties that they can do that on their own and that it does not think they need the court's approval to do so. The Court recommends that the parties talk about mediation, noted that the matter does not qualify for pro bono mediation and stated that it can refer them to the court's mediation panel. Mr. Rodriguez stated that his clients are a bit reluctant to do mediation prior to some discovery being conducted. Mr. Bernhard and Mr. Davis indicated that they have nothing to add. The Petition for Order Reforming Trust, etc. is continued to December 7, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Text Ruling

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Continued To Aug-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez, via ZOOM, counsel for Kathu Liu, Jerry Liu, and Beatrice Hsu, Petitioners; Josh Davis, via ZOOM, counsel for Chuang Mei Liu, Respondent; Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, counsel for Patrick Liu, Jonathan Liu, and Edward Liu, Respondents.

At the outset, the court queries counsel as to the status of the case and why there hasn't been any filings in the case for the past 18 months. Mr. Rodriguez advises that since the pandemic has caused travel restrictions to Taiwan, depositions have been difficult to schedule and complete. The court advises counsel that depositions via remote appearance have been helpful in other matters and encourages counsel to attempt to do the same to assist in moving this matter forward. The court recommends setting Case Management and trial dates in the matter. Counsel agree that setting these dates will help.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Reforming Trust, Invalidating Trust, etc., is continued to August 24, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Additionally, the court orders the following Case Management dates, both at 9:00 am in Department 204: August 24, 2021, and December 07, 2021. This matter is also set for trial on March 01, 2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Kevin Rodriguez is ordered to serve the order Setting Case Management Hearings, Case Management Statements, Setting Trial AND Ordering Joint Trial Plan; Related Orders on opposing counsel as soon as it is available on the court's website.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Jun-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Continued To Mar-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel Bernhard for Patrick Liu, Edward Liu, and Jonathan Liu (Respondents).
Kevin M. Rodriguez for Kathy Liu, Beatrice Shu, and Jerry Liu (Petitioners) via Court Call.
Josh P. Davis for Chuang Mei Liu (Respondent) via Court Call.

The Court inquired as to the discovery before trial. Mr. Bernhard stated written discovery has been propounded and responded to, that some issues are being worked out, that there are discussions regarding depositions, parties are pushing and making progress on deposition schedule, that they hope to take some depositions in Taiwan in February, and depositions of local people by the end of the year. Mr. Rodriguez stated Mr. Bernhard accurately described their discussions and Mr. Davis also agrees with Mr. Bernhard's statements. The Petition for Order 1) Reforming Trust 2) Invalidating Trust Amendment 3) Removing Trustees and Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Suspending Trustee Pending Removal 5) Instructing Trustee re Allocation of Trust Assets 6) re: Liability for Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance is continued to March 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint status report on status of discovery, idea of trial issues, witness list, time estimates, general statement about trial and parties' position regarding mediation to be filed with courtesy copies delivered by March 2, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Aug-05-2019 Continued To Nov-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez appeared by telephone for Kathy Liu and Jerry Beatrice.
Daniel Bernhard appeared by telephone for Edward Liu, Jonathan Liu and Patrick Liu.
Josh Davis appeared by telephone for Chuang mei Liu (Respondent).

Mr. Rodriguez stated that the requests to reform and remove certain provisions are what remain on calendar. The Court noted that it has not reviewed Edward's objections as it was not received timely. Mr. Rodriguez stated that he is presently in the midst of discovery, that he is in the process of preparing responses to written discovery and that it is complicated because there are multiple witnesses in Taiwan and Taipei. Mr. Rodriguez also stated that he will have to set depositions and do some motions, that he is just getting going in the discovery process and that it will take some time (60 to 90-days). Mr. Bernhard stated that most of the witnesses are in Taipei and that he would like to get that going. Mr. Rodriguez spoke of meeting and conferring about working out deposition schedules. Mr. Davis stated that they are not even sure whose depositions are going to be taken and suggested setting the matter out a little further. The Court encouraged the Parties to meet and confer and work on depositions and written discovery. The Petition for Order Reforming Trust, Invalidating Trust Amendment and Liability for Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance (remaining issues in the petition) is continued to November 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects that written discovery will be done, and that the planning and execution of depositions are also done.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Continued To Aug-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez and Mathew John for Petitioners.
Daniel Bernhard for Edward Liu, Patrick Liu and Jonathan Liu (Beneficiaries).
Josh Davis for Chuang Mei Liu.

Mr. Rodriguez stated that he is conducting discovery, that he is attempting to get the drafting Attorney's file and is hoping to convince him to turn it over, and suggested continuing the matter for 60 to 90-days to allow more discovery, and for the parties to come back and see if they are ready for mediation or for trial setting. Mr. Bernhard stated that he will make sure courtesy copies of Edward's objections are provided to the Court. Mr. Davis provided a form of order that appoints Ms. Dolch as Successor Trustee and deems other matters moot as well as dismisses other matters. Mr. Rodriguez stated that he has no objection to the form of order, but he wants to make sure it does not effect all of the other relief requested that remains pending. All Counsel approved the order as to form. The Court will sign the order upon review. The remaining matters in the Petition for Order Reforming Trust, etc. are continued to August 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order: 1)Reforming Trust; 2)Invalidating Trust Amendment;3)Removing Trustees And Appointing Successor Trustee;4)Suspending Trustee Pending Removal; 5)Instructing Trustee Re Allocation Of Trust Assets; And 6)Re: Liability For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Continued To May-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Rodriguez for Kathy Liu, Jerry Liu and Beatrice Hsu (Petitioners).
Daniel Bernhard for Patrick Liu, Jonathan Liu and Edward Liu (Benificiaries).
Ernest Der for Debra Dolch(Successor Trustee).

Mr. Rodriguez stated that Attorney Ross Davis had a family emergency and is unable to attend the hearing. Mr. Rodriguez also stated that Ms. Liu appointed Ms. Dolch as Trustee of the trust and the sub-trust, and that he believes they reached a settlement on allocation of the assets between the Husband's trust and the survivor's trust. In addition, Mr. Rodriguez stated that the request to appoint successor trustee can go off calendar and that allocation of assets is taken care of, suggested continuing the remaining issues to conduct discovery, stated that they can then discuss mediation and that if they can't resolve the matter, set it for trial. Mr. Der confirms that they have reached a settlement on allocation, stated that they have funded the marital trust and the survivor's trust, and that the real estate has been funded and the cash as well. The remaining issues in the Petition for Order Reforming Trust, etc. is continued to May 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further status conference.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
